action.reasoning: Observation: The robot consistently has angle ≈ ±π (human directly behind). With lin_vel=3 + ang_vel=±20, the robot spirals AWAY from human (dist goes from 2.66m to 12.86m). The stuck detection triggers incorrectly. Analysis: When angle ≈ ±π, robot faces AWAY from human. Moving forward while spinning makes it orbit away. Fix: when |angle| > π/2, BACK UP (negative lin_vel) while spinning. This keeps the robot near the human while turning to face it. Also the stuck detection threshold of 0.03m is too tight - human moves so dist changes. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with: 1) Back up when |angle| > π/2 (human behind), 2) Move forward when |angle| < π/2 (human in front), 3) Fix stuck detection. Test with debug script first.
